rationale: My sole round-1 ask (the research §3.2 L3 handoff_log key, absent from loop:) is ADDRESSED via option (a) — the stronger choice. Requirement 4 now declares handoff_log as a loop: config key, string, default AUTOPILOT-LOG.md ('the research-§3.2-L3 morning-handoff path'), marks it inert ('no handoff log is written') consistent with the other declared-not-enforced knobs, and adds handoff_log to the falsification clause. The ZeroConfig AC (line 88) asserts loop.handoff_log="AUTOPILOT-LOG.md"; the populated round-trip AC (line 94) covers the full loop: block. This completes the §3.4 morning-handoff pillar I flagged as the lone unhoused 'stays-human' surface. Overall design fidelity survived the fix round: the four-layer ratchet (R1), the config-selects-authority-never-evidence invariant (R7/R8, config-free leaf + go-list-deps proof), the stays-human floor, and the now-triple-redundant threshold-skip defense (R5 config-side + R7 record-side + R8 gate.go threshold>0 pin) all remain faithful to research §3.2/§3.4 and ADR-0037. The R3.1 reviewer floor (count>=1, sum>=2) closes the single-reviewer always-pass hole faithfully; the R3.3 amendment now honestly reads 'extended, not unchanged'. No new design-fidelity blocker.
findings: ADDRESSED (R1.1) — handoff_log: Requirement 4 (spec.md:52) declares `handoff_log` (string, default AUTOPILOT-LOG.md) as a loop: key alongside gate_authority/halt/budget/context; the falsification clause now names handoff_log; the inert paragraph adds 'no handoff log is written'. ZeroConfig AC (line 88) asserts loop.handoff_log="AUTOPILOT-LOG.md". Key, default, and zero-config coverage all verified. This is the option-(a) resolution — carried as substrate the same way models:/runner: are, matching the spec's own declare-now-so-downstream-consumes principle.

FAITHFUL (survives) — Config-selects-authority-never-evidence intact: PanelGateDecision stays config-input-free (R8), internal/panel imports no internal/config (go list -deps proof, line 101), ReviewerCountNote pure advisory. Unchanged by the fix round.

FAITHFUL (survives) — Stays-human floor + threshold-skip defense strengthened, not weakened: R5 (b) reviewer floor count>=1/sum>=2 (line 53) faithfully closes the [{claude,1}]->'n-1'->0 single-reviewer hole from the reviewer side; R8's 'third-defense pin' (line 56) explicitly forbids deleting the gate.go threshold>0 guard. Three deliberately-redundant defenses (config/record/gate) are a coherent operationalization of research §3.4.

FAITHFUL (survives) — ADR-0037 amendment (lines 36, 55) now reads as an EXTENSION of §3's threshold rule ('does not claim §3 is unchanged: the rule is genuinely extended'), correcting the round-1 'semantically unchanged' framing without touching §§6/8; absent field stays byte-identical N-1.

FAITHFUL (survives) — 109/110/111 boundary, panel/models/runner defaults, and the ADR-0040 four-layer ratchet + portability principle are unchanged from my round-1 assessment and remain faithful to the research; the round-2 edits added ACs (populated round-trip line 94, ADR-0040 content-anchor greps line 95) that harden falsifiability without altering design.

MINOR (non-blocking, unchanged from round 1) — config show render AC (line 93) does not explicitly require handoff_log in the rendered output, but the loop: block is rendered in full and ZeroConfig pins the default, so coverage is adequate; noting only for the implementer.
